name: Walnuts

book_claims.claim: Excellent source of omega-3 alpha-linolenic acid with cardioprotective properties
book_claims.mechanism: 100g provides 9g alpha-linolenic acid, converted to DHA and EPA. These omega-3 reduce inflammation, platelet aggregation, triglycerides. Rich in vitamin E and polyphenols preventing LDL oxidation.

supplementary_research.finding: The WAHA trial (Rajaram et al., 2021; Barcelona cohort) found daily walnut consumption (~30-60g) for 2 years significantly reduced LDL cholesterol and total cholesterol in healthy elderly adults versus control diet.

Prospective analysis from the Nurses' Health Study (Liu et al., JAHA 2020) found consuming 5+ servings of walnuts per week associated with 14% lower mortality risk and ~1.3 years greater life expectancy compared to non-consumers.

Examine rates walnuts as having strong evidence for LDL cholesterol reduction, moderate evidence for improved vascular function and blood pressure, and notable evidence for improved gut microbiome composition and cognitive function.

Meta-analysis (Guasch-Ferre et al., 2017) of 26 clinical trials found walnut-enriched diets significantly decreased total cholesterol, LDL cholesterol, triglycerides, and ApoB compared to control diets.

Meta-analysis of prospective studies (Aune et al., BMC Medicine 2016; updated pooling) found nut consumption (especially walnuts) associated with reduced risk of coronary heart disease, stroke, cardiovascular disease, and all-cause mortality in a dose-response manner.

culinary_pairings.source: Classic European salad and pasta combination
Traditional pairing in baklava and cheese plates
Classic Waldorf salad; autumn fruit-nut pairings
Pesto alla genovese variation; Ligurian walnut sauce
Salsa di noci (Ligurian walnut sauce for pasta)

seasonality: Harvested September-November in Northern Hemisphere. Best fresh (in-shell) in autumn/winter. Available year-round shelled. Store refrigerated or frozen to prevent rancidity of omega-3 oils.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Conversion to EPA is ~5-10%, to DHA ~2-5%. Better absorbed with food. Walnuts are the richest common nut source of ALA.

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: ~1,556 mg gallic acid equivalents (among highest of all nuts)
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Ellagitannins are hydrolyzed to ellagic acid in the gut, then converted by microbiota to urolithins which have anti-inflammatory and anti-aging properties.
Gamma-tocopherol is the predominant form (vs alpha in supplements); has unique anti-inflammatory properties not shared by alpha-tocopherol.
Good bioavailability; partially offset by phytate content which can be reduced by soaking.
Essential for superoxide dismutase (SOD) antioxidant enzyme function
